items.title: python-project-sandbox
items.text: A modern Python project base for experimenting with tooling — powered by uv, Ruff, mypy and pytest, with test coverage and HTML reports published to dedicated branches via GitHub Actions.

items.title: Highlights
items.text:
• Fast tooling — uv for dependency management, Ruff for lint/format, mypy for type checking, just as the task runner.
  • Branch-managed reports — coverage summaries are published to the coverage branch and pytest HTML/cov reports to the ghpages branch. See Reports & coverage for live links and the pytest-html styling setup, and CI & Coverage for the pipeline.
  • Modern tooling, adopted early — zizmor (Actions security), ty (Astral type checker), pip-audit, typos and validate-pyproject run as a preview quality workflow.
  • Multi-OS / multi-Python CI — every change is tested across Ubuntu / macOS / Windows on Python 3.11, 3.12 and 3.13.

items.title: Where to next
items.text:
• Getting started — install dependencies and run the common development tasks.
  • CI & Coverage — how coverage and reports are built and published.

Documentation engine

This site is built with Zensical, the next-generation static site generator from the creators of Material for MkDocs, adopted here as an early-access (preview) tool.

items.title: CI & Coverage
items.text: This project leans on GitHub Actions for testing and for publishing reports to dedicated branches — a core idea of this template.

items.title: Workflows
items.text:
Workflow Trigger Purpose PR Check (Except Dependabot) PR to main Multi-OS / multi-Python tests, 90% coverage gate, coverage PR comment Dependabot PR Check Dependabot PR Same checks for dependency-update PRs Test Multi-OS push to main Tests + publishes the coverage summary to the coverage branch Modern Quality (Preview) PR / push zizmor / ty / pip-audit / typos / validate-pyproject (preview) semantic-release manual (workflow_dispatch) Conventional-Commits release: version bump, CHANGELOG, tag + GitHub Release Docs (Zensical) push / PR Build this site and deploy to ghpages/docs/

All test workflows install just from a prebuilt binary (taiki-e/install-action) and use concurrency to cancel superseded runs, keeping CI fast.

items.title: Branch-managed reports
items.text:
Rather than committing generated artifacts to main, reports live on their own branches:

  • coverage — a generated README.md with the per-OS / per-Python coverage summary. See the coverage branch.
  • ghpages — pytest HTML and coverage reports published to GitHub Pages, driven by pytest-testmon incremental runs. See the reports.

This documentation site is published to the docs/ subdirectory of the ghpages branch, so it coexists with the pytest reports served from the root.

items.title: Releases
items.text: Releases are manually triggered with semantic-release using Conventional Commits (adapted from json2vars-setter). Run the semantic-release workflow from the Actions tab (workflow_dispatch); it then determines the next version from the commits since the last tag, bumps pyproject.toml (via uv version) and uv.lock, updates CHANGELOG.md, commits them back to main ([skip ci]), and creates the vX.Y.Z tag and GitHub Release. Nothing is released until you trigger the workflow.

items.title: Coverage PR comments
items.text: Pull requests receive a coverage comment from pytest-coverage-comment. File links are resolved with coverage-path-prefix: project_a/ so they point at real source paths, and a cleanup_pr_comments job removes stale comments on re-runs to avoid broken links.

items.title: Install
items.text:
This project uses uv for fast Python package management.

ProductionDevelopment 
# Install uv if not already installed
curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh

# Install runtime dependencies
uv sync

# Install dependencies including development tools
uv sync --extra dev

items.title: Task runner (just)
items.text:
Common tasks are wrapped with just:

just --list            # List all available commands
just testcov           # Run tests with coverage
just lint              # Lint with Ruff
just format            # Format with Ruff
just type-check        # Type check with mypy
just check             # Lint + type-check + tests

items.title: Modern tooling (preview)
items.text:
Early-adopted quality tools are also available as just recipes:

just type-check-ty     # Type check with ty (Astral, preview)
just spell             # Spell check with typos
just audit-actions     # GitHub Actions security audit (zizmor)
just audit-deps        # Dependency vulnerability scan (pip-audit)
just validate-pyproject # Validate pyproject.toml schema

items.title: Pre-commit
items.text:
# Install dependencies and the pre-commit hooks
uv sync --extra dev
uv run pre-commit install

# Run all hooks against every file
uv run pre-commit run --all-files

items.title: Build these docs locally
items.text:
The documentation site is built with Zensical:

uvx zensical serve     # Live preview at http://127.0.0.1:8000
uvx zensical build     # Build static site into ./site

items.title: Reports & coverage
items.text: This project keeps report generation independent (each report is produced by a different tool across an OS / Python matrix) but uses this documentation site as the hub that links them together. Nothing is coupled at build time — the docs simply point at the published artifacts.

items.title: What is published, and where
items.text:
Report Tool Location Coverage summary pytest-coverage-comment coverage branch README Coverage HTML pytest-cov ghpages → pytest-cov-report/<os>/python/<version>/ Test report HTML pytest-html ghpages → pytest-html-report/<os>/python/<version>/

The HTML reports are driven by pytest-testmon incremental runs and deployed per OS / Python version. The canonical index — kept up to date with the current matrix — lives in the ghpages branch README.

items.title: Live links
items.text:
• Indexes (recommended entry points):
    • Coverage summary → https://github.com/7rikazhexde/python-project-sandbox/tree/coverage?tab=readme-ov-file#pytest-coverages-summary
    • HTML / cov reports → https://github.com/7rikazhexde/python-project-sandbox/tree/ghpages?tab=readme-ov-file#pytest-report
  • Direct URL pattern (replace <os> / <version>):
    • Test report: …/python-project-sandbox/pytest-html-report/<os>/python/<version>/report_page.html
    • Coverage: …/python-project-sandbox/pytest-cov-report/<os>/python/<version>/index.html
  • Concrete example (ubuntu-latest / Python 3.11.9):
    • Test report
    • Coverage

Why not serve reports from this site?

The reports are large, self-contained HTML generated independently for each of ubuntu / macos / windows × Python 3.12 / 3.13 (and more), using pytest-testmon incremental state. Keeping generation separate avoids coupling two pipelines and bloating the docs build — this page is the hub, the reports stay independent.

items.title: pytest-html styling (custom CSS)
items.text:
The HTML test report is themed with a custom stylesheet at css/style.css. It is applied with pytest-html's --css option, and --self-contained-html inlines the CSS so the published report_page.html is a single portable file:

uv run pytest \
  --html=htmlcov/report_page.html \
  --self-contained-html \
  --css=css/style.css \
  --capture=no \
  project_a tests/

Or via the task runner:

just testhtml          # -> htmlcov/report_page.html (styled)

In CI, the same --css=css/style.css is passed in test_pytest-html-report_deploy_multi_os.yml before the report is published to ghpages.

Reproduce this setup in your own project

  1. Add a stylesheet (e.g. css/style.css).
  2. Generate the report with --html=… --self-contained-html --css=css/style.css.
  3. Publish the per-matrix output to a ghpages subdirectory (this project uses pytest-<html|cov>-report/<os>/python/<version>/), keeping existing files so the reports and these docs coexist.
  4. Link everything from a single hub page — like this one.
